candidate_problems.text: How can the proposed invariants be made precise enough to distinguish genuine concept formation from mere robust correlation? In particular, what operational criteria would separate reidentifiability, constraint sensitivity, error visibility, and revision-without-collapse from sophisticated statistical pattern matching that only appears invariant-preserving?

What is the minimal architecture or set of mechanisms required for meaning to emerge under this conjecture, and are the listed invariants jointly necessary or merely sufficient? This includes whether stable semantics can arise from prediction-error correction alone, from world-model structure alone, or only from a specific feedback loop between invariance preservation and corrigibility.

How does the conjecture ground reference and normativity rather than only internal coherence? A system may preserve its own invariants and correct its own errors yet still drift away from reality or shared public meaning, so the open question is what links internally stable, self-correcting representations to successful aboutness in the world and across agents.
